_note: Synthetic lethal and induced dependency map for loss of function drivers. A tumor suppressor cannot be drugged directly, but its loss creates a dependency on a partner gene that can be. For a loss driver, Anveshar validates and targets the induced dependency (the partner), not the lost gene. Every pair carries a real citation. Research use, not medical advice.

SMARCB1.relationship: SWI/SNF (SMARCB1) loss shifts chromatin control to the PRC2/EZH2 complex, creating an EZH2 dependency

citation.label: Gounder et al., Lancet Oncol 2020 (tazemetostat in INI1/SMARCB1 negative epithelioid sarcoma proves the EZH2 dependency)

ARID1A.relationship: ARID1A (SWI/SNF) mutation creates a synthetic lethal dependency on EZH2 methyltransferase activity

citation.label: Bitler et al., Nat Med 2015 (EZH2 inhibition is synthetic lethal in ARID1A mutated cancer)

SMARCA4.relationship: loss of the SWI/SNF ATPase SMARCA4 (BRG1) creates a dependency on its paralog SMARCA2 (BRM)

citation.label: Ehrenhofer-Wolfer et al., Sci Rep 2019 (SWI/SNF ATPase paralog synthetic lethality)

MTAP.relationship: MTAP deletion (often co-deleted with CDKN2A) raises MTA and creates a selective dependency on PRMT5

citation.label: Briggs et al., 2025 (MTA cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor for MTAP deleted cancers)

VHL.relationship: VHL loss stabilizes HIF-2 alpha, creating a HIF-2 alpha dependency (pseudohypoxia)

citation.label: Choueiri et al., 2021 (belzutifan targeting HIF-2 alpha in VHL disease associated tumors)
